metadata.notes: Generated by Protocol Intelligence Platform. SR- IDs are placeholders.

general_information.notes: To be completed by Risk Manager upon IRMS entry

study_overview.title: TACTIVE-U: AN INTERVENTIONAL SAFETY AND EFFICACY PHASE 1B/2, OPEN-LABEL UMBRELLA STUDY TO INVESTIGATE TOLERABILITY, PK, AND ANTITUMOR ACTIVITY OF VEPDEGESTRANT (ARV-471/PF-07850327), AN ORAL PROTEOLYSIS TARGETING CHIMERA, IN COMBINATION WITH OTHER ANTICANCER TREATMENTS IN PARTICIPANTS AGED 18 AND OLDER WITH ER+ ADVANCED OR METASTATIC BREAST CANCER, SUB-STUDY C (ARV-471 IN COMBINATION WITH SAMURACICLIB)

critical_factors.critical_data: Dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) in Cycle 1; Confirmed objective response rate (ORR), DoR, CBR, and PFS per RECIST v1.1; PK concentrations for ARV-471, ARV-473, and samuraciclib; Biomarker data from blood and tumor tissue.
critical_factors.critical_process: Investigator assessment of tumor response per RECIST v1.1; Accurate observation and reporting of DLTs during the Cycle 1 DLT observation period; Timely collection of PK and biomarker samples.

critical_data.signal: EP_SC_04_ORR - Confirmed objective response rate per RECIST v1.1

critical_process.signal: Assess clinical antitumor activity by investigator-assessed confirmed ORR

critical_factors.critical_data: AE/SAE incidence and counts; Clinically significant laboratory abnormalities; ECG abnormalities (QTcF prolongation).
critical_factors.critical_process: Timely AE/SAE reporting and reconciliation; Continuous safety monitoring including ECGs and laboratory assessments; Verification of highly effective contraception compliance.

critical_data.signal: EP_SC_01_AEincidence - Incidence and counts of AEs and SAEs

critical_process.signal: Highly effective contraceptive method requirement for IOCBP

critical_factors.factor_name: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results
critical_factors.critical_data: Tumor assessment scans; Concomitant medication logs; PK/PD sample collection times relative to dosing.
critical_factors.critical_process: Consistent scan modality completed for disease response assessment; Proper handling, processing, and shipment of PK and biomarker samples; Strict adherence to prohibited medication washouts.

critical_data.signal: Prohibited concurrent administration of strong CYP3A/CYP2D6 inhibitors
EP_SC_08_PKconc - Plasma concentrations over specified PK sampling times

critical_process.signal: Open-label design requiring strict adherence to RECIST v1.1 for unbiased assessment

critical_factors.critical_data: Prior lines of therapy (chemotherapy, endocrine therapies, CDK4/6 inhibitors); eGFR values; Hepatic function tests (AST, ALT, Bilirubin); Tumor ER+/HER2- status.
critical_factors.critical_process: Accurate verification of prior therapy lines (exactly 1 prior CDK4/6 inhibitor required); Confirmation of adequate renal and hepatic function; Verification of appropriate washout periods for prior therapies and prohibited medications.

critical_data.signal: 1, and only 1, line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen required
Renal impairment defined by an eGFR < 45 mL/min

critical_process.signal: Phase 2: must have received an aromatase inhibitor in combination with a CDK4/6 inhibitor

critical_factors.critical_data: Dosing records for ARV-471 and samuraciclib; Dose modifications, interruptions, or discontinuations due to toxicity.
critical_factors.critical_process: Administration of ARV-471 with food and samuraciclib with or without food; Management of continuous 28-day cycles; Accurate dose level assignment based on Phase 1b escalation decisions.

critical_data.signal: Continuous 28-day cycles for both ARV-471 and samuraciclib

critical_process.signal: ARV-471 administered continuously on 28-day cycles and with food

risks.risk_description: Enrolled participants may not meet the complex prior therapy requirements or strict organ function cutoffs.
risks.additional_context: The protocol has highly specific prior therapy rules, including exactly 1 prior CDK4/6 inhibitor, ≤1 prior chemotherapy, and specific combinations (aromatase inhibitor + CDK4/6 inhibitor) for Phase 2. Additionally, strict eGFR and hepatic function cutoffs must be met.

associated_causes.cause: Misinterpretation of prior lines of therapy by site staff.

usdm_trigger.signal: Up to 2 lines of prior therapies... 1, and only 1, line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor

associated_causes.cause: Inaccurate calculation of eGFR or misinterpretation of hepatic function lab results.

usdm_trigger.signal: Renal impairment defined by an eGFR < 45 mL/min

usdm_drivers.signal: Complex prior therapy and lab value exclusions

controls.control_description: Implement EDC edit checks for critical inclusion/exclusion criteria, including eGFR calculation and prior therapy counts.
controls.additional_context: Focus on CDK4/6 inhibitor history and washout periods.

controls.control_description: Medical monitor review of participant eligibility prior to enrollment, especially for Phase 2 and DDI cohorts.
controls.additional_context: Ensure prior therapy lines and washout periods are strictly verified.

intelligence.notes: The protocol has highly specific prior therapy rules, including exactly 1 prior CDK4/6 inhibitor, ≤1 prior chemotherapy, and specific combinations (aromatase inhibitor + CDK4/6 inhibitor) for Phase 2.

risks.risk_description: Participants may take prohibited strong/moderate CYP3A, CYP2D6, or CYP2C19 inhibitors/inducers, or QT-prolonging drugs.
risks.additional_context: The study involves investigational drugs with significant DDI potential. The protocol strictly prohibits concurrent administration of strong CYP inhibitors/inducers and requires specific washout periods (7-14 days) prior to enrollment, with even stricter rules for the DDI Assessment Cohort.

associated_causes.cause: Participant uses over-the-counter herbal supplements or medications without informing the investigator.

usdm_trigger.signal: Concurrent administration of medications, food, or herb supplements that are strong inhibitors/inducers

associated_causes.cause: Investigator prescribes a prohibited medication for an AE without checking the prohibited list.

usdm_trigger.signal: Prior use of strong CYP3A and CYP2D6 inhibitors must be stopped 7 days before enrollment

critical_factors_linked: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results

controls.control_description: EDC edit checks to flag the entry of prohibited concomitant medications.
controls.additional_context: Cross-reference with the protocol's Appendix 9 list of prohibited drugs.

controls.control_description: Provide participants with a prohibited medication card to present to other healthcare providers.
controls.additional_context: Emphasize the risk of herbal supplements and specific antibiotics/antifungals.

intelligence.notes: The study involves investigational drugs with significant DDI potential. The protocol strictly prohibits concurrent administration of strong CYP inhibitors/inducers and requires specific washout perio

risks.risk_description: PK and biomarker samples (blood and tumor tissue) may be incorrectly collected, processed, or shipped.
risks.additional_context: The study requires intensive PK sampling for three different analytes (ARV-471, ARV-473, samuraciclib) and collection of peripheral blood and archival/fresh tumor tissue for exploratory biomarker analysis.

associated_causes.cause: Missed or incorrectly timed PK draws relative to dosing.

usdm_trigger.signal: Plasma concentrations of ARV-471, ARV-473, and samuraciclib measured over specified PK sampling times

associated_causes.cause: Improper handling or storage of tumor tissue blocks or blood biospecimens.

usdm_trigger.signal: Measurements of biomarkers from peripheral blood and/or tumor tissue biospecimens

critical_factors_linked: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results

controls.control_description: Site training on the laboratory manual, specifically focusing on PK sampling windows and tumor tissue processing.
controls.additional_context: Ensure sites understand the distinct processing requirements for the three PK analytes.

controls.control_description: Routine monitoring of PK sample collection logs and shipment records.
controls.additional_context: Verify that actual collection times are accurately recorded in the EDC.

intelligence.notes: The study requires intensive PK sampling for three different analytes (ARV-471, ARV-473, samuraciclib) and collection of peripheral blood and archival/fresh tumor tissue for exploratory biomarker anal

risks.risk_description: Lack of appropriate or timely reporting of Dose-Limiting Toxicities (DLTs) or other SAEs during the combination treatment.
risks.additional_context: As a Phase 1b/2 study evaluating a novel combination (ARV-471 + samuraciclib), accurate and timely DLT reporting in Cycle 1 is critical for dose selection and overall participant safety.

associated_causes.cause: Site staff fail to report late SAEs within 24 hours.

usdm_trigger.signal: Incidence and counts of adverse events (AEs) and serious adverse events (SAEs)

associated_causes.cause: Misclassification or delayed reporting of a DLT by the investigator during Cycle 1.

usdm_trigger.signal: Occurrence of dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) during the Cycle 1 DLT observation period

usdm_drivers.signal: Assess safety and tolerability to select recommended dose levels

controls.control_type: Monitors reinforce requirements to report late SAEs within 24 hours (per INV-GSOP-SD-GL)
controls.control_description: CRAs to reinforce 24-hour SAE reporting requirements during routine monitoring visits.
controls.additional_context: Special emphasis on DLT reporting timelines during Cycle 1.

controls.control_description: Frequent medical monitor review of safety data, particularly during the Phase 1b dose escalation phase.
controls.additional_context: Real-time review of Cycle 1 safety data to identify potential DLTs.

intelligence.notes: As a Phase 1b/2 study evaluating a novel combination (ARV-471 + samuraciclib), accurate and timely DLT reporting in Cycle 1 is critical for dose selection and overall participant safety.
